Alex has always held traditional beliefs about love and relationships. Recently, they have begun exploring novels that offer diverse perspectives. These stories have challenged their long-held views. The first novel Alex read had 120 pages. It took them 4 days to finish. Each day, they read a consistent number of pages. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages. It required 6 days to complete. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages. It took 8 days to finish. 

Alex read the same number of pages each day for every novel. This consistency is key to calculating their daily reading rate. To find the average pages read per day, we must consider all three novels. First, we calculate the pages read per day for each book. Then, we find the average across all three. This method ensures accuracy. The first novel’s daily reading rate is 120 divided by 4. That equals 30 pages per day. The second novel’s rate is 180 divided by 6. That equals 30 pages per day. The third novel’s rate is 240 divided by 8. That also equals 30 pages per day. 

Since all three novels had the same daily reading rate, the average remains 30 pages per day. This consistency is surprising. It shows Alex’s dedication to reading. Each novel required a different number of days. But the pages per day stayed the same. This is due to the proportional increase in pages and days. The first novel had fewer pages and fewer days. The second novel had more pages and more days. The third novel had the most pages and the most days. 

The pattern is clear. The number of pages increased by 60 each time. The number of days also increased by 2 each time. This means the daily reading rate remained constant. Alex’s reading pace did not change. This is remarkable. It shows discipline and focus. Each novel was a challenge. But Alex met each challenge with the same effort. 

The first novel was a test of patience. It required reading 30 pages each day. The second novel was a test of endurance. It also required 30 pages per day. The third novel was a test of commitment. It also required 30 pages per day. Alex’s consistency is impressive. It reflects a strong reading habit. Each novel was different in content and length. But the daily reading rate stayed the same.
